creator: linimon@FreeBSD.org
text:
Note: with portmgr hat, I've committed the following emailed changes
from the maintainer to make the port work for release.  However, I have
held off on the upgrade for now due to the freeze.

----------  Forwarded Message  ----------

Subject: Re: status of FreeBSD port security/sfs
Date: Monday 19 November 2007
From: Dave Grochowski <malus.x@gmail.com>
To: Mark Linimon <linimon@lonesome.com>

Sorry to bother you, but the change recently made does not properly fix 
security/sfs. Clients cannot make connections without sfsrwcd. The 
attached patch (which is just a portion of the PR) should fix the issue. 
Would it be possible to get the patch committed before 7.0/6.3 is 
released? Thanks!

Sincerely,
Dave Grochowski

-------------------------------------------------------
